hook: AI gave me a 6-paragraph essay when I asked what to have for lunch.
caption:
AI gave me a 6-paragraph essay when I asked what to have for lunch.

No joke. I said 'I'm not that hungry, what should I eat?' and got a structured breakdown with headers. Nutritional considerations. A reminder to 'listen to my body.'

I just wanted someone to say 'grab a yogurt and some almonds, you'll be fine.'

Here's the thing — AI doesn't know that you're a 'just tell me the answer' person. It doesn't know you hate preamble. It doesn't know that when you ask a small question, you want a small answer.

So it defaults to thorough. Comprehensive. Covering all its bases.

Which feels like asking your friend for directions and they hand you a laminated map.

Your thinking style is specific. Some people WANT the 6 paragraphs — they're the ones who feel anxious without full context. Others need the one-liner or they'll never act on it.

BootFile figures out which one you are. Then every AI interaction stops feeling like it was written for someone else.

The yogurt answer exists. You just need AI that knows you well enough to give it to you.
